Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The process description mentions a policy that gives a slight upward adjustment to applicants who voluntarily list membership in the Highland Civic Darts Club or provide a local address verified through public records

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is a rule based adjustment The bias is introduced in step 3

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Also in step 4 underwriters may subconsciously view applicants with community engagement more favorably

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: The policy is not based on protected characteristics but it might still be problematic if it systematically disadvantages certain groups

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This could be seen as unfair because it rewards something unrelated to creditworthiness

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: Applicants who are not from the local region or do not have such affiliations get no boost meaning they must meet baseline thresholds

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Possibly justifiable if there is evidence that community integration correlates with lower default risk but it says perceived though not formally proven

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So it is not evidence based it is a policy based on perception That is problematic

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Also it is not transparent not disclosed Could lead to discrimination against those who are not local or not in such clubs Even if not legally protected it is still unfair to base lending decisions on arbitrary community ties

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also the process may indirectly discriminate against protected groups if those groups are less likely to be long term residents or club members
